reference: My daughter is covered by PeachCare. She has a problem with obsessive-compulsive disorder and delusional issues. She washes her hands and her feet thirty to fifty times a day, lies constantly, sees things that are not really happening, and believes there are bugs crawling on her. I am looking for help that my insurance will cover, but not just someone who is going to give her medication and leave her on her own. I believe this needs to be more in-depth care. What do you think?
text: My daughter is covered by peach care. She has a problem with obsessive-compulsive disorder and delusional issues; she washes her hands and her feet 30 to 50 times a day, lies constantly, sees things that are not really happening, and believes there are bugs crawling on her. I am looking for help that my insurance will cover, but not just someone who is going to give her medication and leave her on her own. I believe this needs to be more in-depth care. What do you think?
